title: OpenAI says AI investment should be measured by useful work per dollar
friendlyTitle: OpenAI says: measure useful AI work, not just token cost
summary: OpenAI has published a five-step framework for managing AI spend as organisations move from chat to longer-running workflows. It argues that leaders should measure accepted outcomes, including human review and retries, rather than token prices alone.
friendlySummary: OpenAI shares five steps for managing AI spending. Its main idea is to measure useful finished work, not only the price of AI tokens.
whyItMatters: The practical shift is from buying model access to managing repeatable work: visibility, task-specific evaluations, approval paths and capacity planning become central as agents touch more business systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help teams spend money on AI that really saves time or improves work, instead of paying for tools that do not deliver enough value.
